expectations.text: recommendations.md discusses naming with explicit reference to grep-ability, retrieval, or localization
expectations.evidence: Section 1 says 'Agents search by filename and pattern' and Section 4 prescribes greppable names.

expectations.text: recommendations.md recommends organizing code by feature / vertical slice rather than horizontal layer
expectations.evidence: Section 1 shows src/users/ feature directory and Section 6 says 'Split by feature, not by layer within a feature.'

expectations.text: recommendations.md warns against metaprogramming, reflection, or dynamic dispatch
expectations.evidence: Section 13 'Prefer plain data structures over clever abstractions' warns against decorators, Proxy, and runtime metaprogramming.

expectations.text: recommendations.md recommends typed public boundaries / type annotations at module seams
expectations.evidence: Section 2 colocates contracts with implementations and Section 17 mandates schemas at boundaries; Section 20 requires strict TypeScript.

expectations.text: recommendations.md recommends verification affordances tied to preventing agent hallucination spirals
expectations.evidence: Section 12 'Make how to run it trivial' addresses tool commands but does not tie verification loops to preventing hallucination spirals or wrong-patch loops.

expectations.text: recommendations.md addresses file size, near-duplicates, or str_replace breakage
expectations.evidence: Section 6 soft-caps files at 300 lines and flags the agent-missing-cross-references failure, though does not name str_replace.

expectations.text: recommendations.md grounds reasoning in specific agent failure modes rather than generic clean-code platitudes
expectations.evidence: Failure modes are described generically ('agent patches wrong layer', 'hallucinate type signatures') without naming context-window limits, str_replace, lost-in-the-middle, or specific documented spirals.

expectations.text: recommendations.md presents the list in a prioritized order
expectations.evidence: Explicit Tier 1 through Tier 4 ordering by impact.

expectations.text: recommendations.md covers deterministic tests (seeded randomness, frozen time) OR injectable side effects — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 22 mentions seeds and fixtures in passing but does not prescribe seeded randomness, frozen time, or injectable clocks/side effects for deterministic tests.

expectations.text: recommendations.md mentions branded/nominal types OR discriminated unions over magic strings — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: No mention of branded/nominal types; discriminated unions are not recommended as an alternative to magic strings anywhere in the document.

expectations.text: recommendations.md flags barrel re-export files or 'export *' as an anti-pattern — v1.3 guidance (AR011)
expectations.evidence: Section 7 caps barrel files at one level and prefers direct imports over barrel index.ts re-exports.

expectations.text: recommendations.md mentions dependency pinning OR training-data / 'boring stack' considerations — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 16 'Pin and document external contracts' explicitly pins exact versions for network-boundary packages.
